questions.question: What is the primary purpose of an Intent in Android?
options: A) To send data between activities
B) To receive data from other activities
C) To start a new activity
questions.explanation: An Intent is used to request an action from an app component. It can be used to start a new activity, send data between activities, or receive data from other activities.

questions.question: What type of Intent filter is used to filter incoming Intents?
questions.explanation: Component filter is used to filter incoming Intents to ensure they reach the correct component of an app.

questions.question: At what stage of the Activity life cycle does the 'onCreate' method get called?
questions.explanation: The 'onCreate' method gets called when the Activity is created and before any views are created.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'onStart' method in the Activity life cycle?
questions.explanation: The 'onStart' method gets called when the Activity is started and before any views are created.

questions.question: At what stage of the Broadcast life cycle does the 'onReceive' method get called?
questions.explanation: The 'onReceive' method gets called when the broadcast is received by the system and the component has requested to receive it.
